metadata.description: Unified registry of researchers, institutions, standards, and legislation relevant to QIF neurosecurity research. Cross-references QIF-RESEARCH-SOURCES.md IDs and references.bib keys.

researchers.name: Tadayoshi Kohno
researchers.qifRelevance: Coined 'neurosecurity' in 2009. Foundational BCI security research.

researchers.name: Ivan Martinovic
researchers.qifRelevance: First EEG side-channel attack demonstration (USENIX 2012).

researchers.name: Tamara Bonaci
researchers.qifRelevance: BCI app store privacy model. Subliminal image attack on BCI.

researchers.name: Marcello Ienca
researchers.qifRelevance: Neurorights pioneer. Co-authored foundational neurorights paper (2017). BCI security lifecycle survey.

researchers.name: Rafael Yuste
researchers.qifRelevance: Four ethical priorities for neurotech (Nature 2017). Neurorights Foundation founder. Chile neurorights advisor.

researchers.name: Nita Farahany
researchers.qifRelevance: Battle for Your Brain (2023). Leading voice on cognitive liberty and neural data rights.

researchers.name: Sara Goering
researchers.qifRelevance: IEEE Neuroethics Framework contributor. Responsible BCI development guidelines.

researchers.name: Gabriel Lazaro-Munoz
researchers.qifRelevance: DBS post-trial access ethics. Device abandonment concerns.

researchers.name: Lubin Meng
researchers.institution: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
researchers.qifRelevance: EEG adversarial robustness benchmark. Systematic adversarial ML applied to BCI.

researchers.name: Tyler Schroder
researchers.qifRelevance: Most comprehensive BCI security paper (2025). Neuroethics journal publication.

researchers.name: Deborah J.H. Mathews
researchers.institution: Johns Hopkins Berman Institute of Bioethics
researchers.qifRelevance: Three-tiered governance framework for emerging tech. QIF as Tier 3 enforcement tool.

researchers.name: Juan Lopez Bernal
researchers.qifRelevance: BCI security taxonomy papers. 4 TARA techniques derived from taxonomy gap analysis.

researchers.name: Song Liu
researchers.qifRelevance: Professor X invisible backdoor attack on EEG BCI (2024). Novel invisible trigger mechanism.

researchers.name: Yanjie Kong
researchers.qifRelevance: Brainprint adversarial attacks (ICME 2024). EEG biometric spoofing.

researchers.name: Xue Jiang
researchers.institution: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
researchers.qifRelevance: CNS+PNS neural interface cybersecurity survey (2023). Comprehensive threat landscape.

researchers.name: Judy Illes
researchers.qifRelevance: UBC National Core for Neuroethics director. Pioneer in neuroimaging ethics and Indigenous neuroscience ethics.

researchers.name: Karen Rommelfanger
researchers.qifRelevance: Only US PhD concentration in neuroethics. Center for Neuroimaging and Neuroethics director.

researchers.name: Winston Chiong
researchers.qifRelevance: UCSF Decision Lab. NIH BRAIN Initiative neuroethics. DBS informed consent research.

researchers.name: Laura Cabrera
researchers.qifRelevance: IEEE P7700 Neurotechnology Standards chair. Public attitudes toward neurotechnology research.

researchers.name: Herve Chneiweiss
researchers.qifRelevance: Co-chair of UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group on Neurotechnology (2025). French neuroscience policy leadership.

researchers.name: Amy McGuire
researchers.qifRelevance: Neural data consent frameworks. Genomic-neural ethics intersection.

researchers.name: Georg Kellmeyer
researchers.qifRelevance: Mental privacy and integrity conceptual analysis. Highlighted underspecification of core neuroethics concepts.

researchers.name: Christoph Bublitz
researchers.qifRelevance: Mental freedom and cognitive liberty framework. Anti-inflationism perspective on neurorights.

researchers.name: Eric Racine
researchers.qifRelevance: Neuro-realism, neuro-essentialism, neuro-policy triad (2005). Foundational neuroethics rigor.

researchers.name: Russell Poldrack
researchers.qifRelevance: Reverse inference fallacy (2006). Foundational methodological constraint on brain imaging claims.

researchers.name: Michael Tennison
researchers.qifRelevance: Neuroethics and neurorights integration. Dual-use warning: security framing risks enabling surveillance.

institutions.fullName: The MITRE Corporation

standards.name: CVSS v4.0
standards.scope: General vulnerability scoring with extension framework
standards.qifRelevance: NISS extends CVSS v4.0 Extension Framework for neural-specific metrics

standards.name: MITRE ATT&CK
standards.qifRelevance: TARA taxonomy follows ATT&CK structure (tactics to techniques). No neural-specific coverage.

standards.name: MITRE CWE
standards.qifRelevance: No CWE entries for BCI/neural interface weaknesses. Gap identified.

standards.name: ISO 14971:2019
standards.qifRelevance: General medical device risk framework. No BCI-specific threat categories.

standards.name: IEC 62443
standards.qifRelevance: Industrial security zones/conduits model. Adaptable to BCI but no neural coverage.

standards.name: FDORA Section 524B
standards.qifRelevance: First mandatory cybersecurity requirements for medical devices including BCIs. TARA mapped to all provisions.

standards.name: EU MDR 2017/745
standards.qifRelevance: EU medical device framework. Cybersecurity mentioned but not BCI-specific.

standards.name: EU AI Act (2024/1689)
standards.scope: AI systems regulation including high-risk classification for neurotech
standards.qifRelevance: Classifies neurotechnology as high-risk AI. Requires conformity assessments.

standards.name: UNESCO Recommendation on Ethics of Neurotechnology
standards.qifRelevance: First international ethical framework for neurotechnology. Adopted at 43rd General Conference.

standards.name: OECD Recommendation on Responsible Innovation in Neurotechnology
standards.qifRelevance: First intergovernmental standard on neurotechnology governance. 9 principles.

standards.name: IEEE P2731
standards.qifRelevance: Standardized BCI/BMI terminology. Foundational for cross-framework communication.

standards.name: IEEE P2794
standards.qifRelevance: Brain data interoperability. Complements QIF data governance model.

standards.name: IEEE P7700
standards.qifRelevance: Neurotechnology-specific IEEE standard. Cabrera chairs the working group.

standards.name: Council of Europe Strategic Action Plan on Neurotechnology
standards.scope: Human rights framework for neurotechnology across 46 member states
standards.qifRelevance: Human rights-based neurotech governance for 46 countries.

legislation.name: Colorado HB 24-1058 (Neural Data as Sensitive Data)
Latin American Parliament Model Law on Neurorights
UN Human Rights Council Neurotech Mandate
EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) — Neurotech as High-Risk
Global Privacy Assembly 46th Conference Resolution on Neural Data
